case_title: JOHN DOE VS. DAVID B. YEARY

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE UNDER PSEUDONYM IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 501 TO BE HEARD ON MAY 14, 2026. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE UNDER PSEUDONYM IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SUPPORT THE MOTION WITH SUFFICIENT FACTS AND EVIDENCE ESTABLISHING AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO COURT RECORDS, AND A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY THAT INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF A PSEUDONYM IS NOT USED. CONCLUSORY FEARS OF REPUTATIONAL AND ECONOMIC INJURY DO NOT OUTWEIGH THE PUBLIC'S SUBSTANTIAL AND PRESUMPTIVE INTEREST IN DISCLOSURE. (ROE V. SMITH (2025) 116 CAL.APP.5TH 227, 244.) JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: K. VALDES; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Under Pseudonym
Off Calendar May-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to File Under Pseudonym is DENIED without prejudice. Plaintiff failed to support the motion with sufficient facts and evidence establishing an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to court records, and a substantial probability that interest will be prejudiced if a pseudonym is not used. Conclusory fears of reputational and economic injury do not outweigh the public's substantial and presumptive interest in disclosure. (Roe v. Smith (2025) 116 Cal.App.5th 227, 244.)

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

Note: Stipulation filed on May-12-2026 to continue hearing on plaintiff's motion for leave to file under pseudonym was received by the court after the tentative ruling was issued on May-13-2026.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Under Pseudonym
Off Calendar Apr-30-2026 Continued To May-14-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE UNDER PSEUDONYM

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: This is a real property matter. The motion is transferred to Department 501 to be heard on May 14, 2026. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)
